Wirkungsmonitoring Innosuisse
Fragebogen Internationale Projektförderung
Implementations- und Forschungspartner

text.text:
Innosuisse / Eureka

Survey of Swiss Eurostars project partners


{{member.MonthsSinceEnd}} months after project completion

Thank you for taking the time to fill in the survey!

About {{member.MonthsSinceEnd}} months ago, you completed your international innovation project «{{member.Titel}}» (project number: {{member.ExterneID}}), which Innosuisse supported with funding.
This survey asks about the type of innovation, the current status of your project and the results and impacts achieved since completion.

Whenever we use the term organisation below, we mean your company, administrative unit or institution.

Completing the questionnaire takes about 20 minutes.

functionTerm.text: (IUnternehmenart == 2 or iOrgRole == 2) ? "Please refer to the employees of the administration office or hospital department involved in the project." : "If your organisation is part of a holding or a group: Please indicate the required information at the level of the smallest, legally independent organisation, and not at holding/group level."

IKontaktCheck_1_1 = member.LastName
IKontaktCheck_2_1 = member.FirstName
IKontaktCheck_3_1 = member.EMail
IKontaktCheck_4_1 = member.ExterneID
IKontaktCheck_5_1 = member.Titel

text.text: Please check the following information about yourself and your innovation project and correct it if necessary.

hintText.text:
<style type="text/css">
	.rt-container .rt-matrix-container table .rt-mtx-choice-has-open label + .rt-mtx-open-container {
		width: 200%
	}

</style>

text.text: What was the role of your organisation in the project?

hintText.text:
• An implementation partner ensures practical applicability of the project and/or has the capacity and intent to implement or commercialize the project outcomes effectively.
  • A research partner contributes scientifically to the project.

text.text: a public enterprise or an institution assigned to the public administration (incl. public hospitals)
another type of organisation (e.g. NGO, NPO, association, foundation)
Is your organisation ...
Please indicate the year of foundation of your organisation according to the commercial register.

hintText.text: Please enter a four-digit year.

text.text: Do you consider your organisation to be a start-up?

hintText.text: Key criteria for start-ups: Typically, no more than 10 years old (exceptions are possible), science- and/or technology-based, scalable business model, international orientation

text.text: ... did your organisation have at the end of the project, i.e. at the end of {{member.AbschlussJahr}}
... does your organisation have today, i.e. at the end of the last completed business year
How many staff...

hintText.text:
FTEs (number of full-time equivalents)

State the number of FTEs within your organisation during the specified financial periods:
  • Anyone who worked fulltime within your organisation (or on its behalf) for the entire year counts as one unit. 
  • Treat part time staff, seasonal workers and those who did not work the full year as fractions of one unit. 

Headcount (number of employees)

This includes the following full time, part time and seasonal staff: employees, those working for your organisation and considered to be employees according to national law, owner-managers and o partners engaged in a regular activity with your organisation and benefiting from financial advantages from your organisation.
Do not include apprentices or students engaged in vocational training (with apprenticeship or vocational training contracts) or employees on maternity or parental leave in the headcount.
Example: Nine people are employed in a company. Five of them work full-time and four occupy a part-time (half-time) position. The headcount is 9. The FTE is 7.

{{custom.TextFTEUnternehmenAmt}}

Please use a point as decimal separator.

text.text: Yes, our participation in the project aimed to implement the innovation in our organisation or on the market.
No, we were involved in the project as another implementation partner.
Does or did your organisation pursue the goal of implementing a concrete innovation in the organisation or on the market with the supported project?

hintText.text: Innovations refer to significant improvements or new developments. Innovations include product and service innovations, process innovations as well as innovations in the business model, in the organisation or in marketing.

text.text: Have you addressed the differing needs and challenges of women and men (with the goal of eliminating inequalities and promoting gender equality) in the design, methodologies or results of your project?

hintText.text: These kind of activities could involve setting research priorities and avoiding gender-bias in the outcome of your project e.g., in defining concepts, formulating research questions, developing methodologies, gathering and analysing gender disaggregated data or evaluating the products, processes or services and transferring them to markets.

placeholder.text: Bitte geben Sie hier Ihre Antwort ein.

text.text: Which aspects featured a gender dimension and how were they incorporated?

hintText.text: The activities could have involved the setting of research priorities, avoiding gender-bias in the outcome e.g., through defining concepts, formulating research questions, developing methodologies, gathering and analysing sex/gender disaggregated data, evaluating and reporting results and transferring them to markets into products and innovations.

text.text: Innovation implementation and market entry
Yes, they have already entered the market.
Yes, they have already been implemented (but not entered the market) and the supported project has since been completed.
Yes, they have already been implemented and the supported project will continue.
Yes, they will be implemented in the next 24 months.
No, but implementation is planned for later.
No implementation is planned, but the supported innovation idea will be continued in a follow-up project.
No, the supported project will not be continued.
Have any of the project results already been implemented in the market, in society or in your organisation, or is this implementation planned within the next 24 months?
Which existing geographical markets (if any) have your products, processes and services entered as a result of your participation in the project?
Which new geographical markets (if any) have your products, processes and services entered as a result of your participation in the project?
The product or service is not competitive
Certification or other regulatory approval is needed
What were the reasons the products, processes or services you developed in your project did not enter a market?
Non-legal and technical advice (e.g., support developing a business plan, obtaining expert technical inputs)
Financial advice (e.g., on taxation or other subsidy schemes)
Access to private investors (e.g., corporate equity, business angels)
What additional support would have been useful during or after the lifetime of the project for bringing your products, processes and services to the market?
Characterisation of the supported project
The implementation and research partners in equal parts
Who was primarily responsible for developing the innovation idea and submitting the application?
What type of innovation is the supported project?

hintText.text: Please tick everything that applies.

text.text: How many distinct products, processes and services were developed by your organisation as a result of your project?
New product, process or service in one of your existing market(s)
New product, process, or service for your organisation
Improvement of an existing product, process or service
Select all categories that apply to your project results:
technological or natural science-based results (e.g. engineering, ICT, life sciences)
results from social sciences, humanities or economic sciences
Is the innovation from your project primarily based on ...

notAvailableChoiceText.text: Don't know / I cannot answer this question

text.text: It is based on new digital technologies ...
It is based on new digital business models ...
Is the Innosuisse-supported project based on new digital technologies or new digital business models?

hintText.text:
Examples of digital technologiesExamples of digital business models
  • Cloud Computing, Cloud sourcing
  • Virtual Reality, Augmented Reality
  • Virtual Factory, Digital Twin
  • Internet of Things (IoT)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI), Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Neural Networks
  • Robotics
  • Distributed Ledger Technologies (e.g. Blockchain)
  • Quantum Computing
  • Software as a Service SaaS / Platform as a Service PaaS
  • Infrastructure as a Service - E-Commerce, Marketplace Digital Service Provider (On-Demand, Subscription, Freemium, etc.)
  • Crowdsourcing and Crowdfunding - Big Data business model

text.text: The innovation pursued by the project is ...
Innovations can be incremental or radical in nature. Please indicate which applies to your innovation pursued with the project.

hintText.text:
Incremental innovations are further developments and optimisations of existing products, services, processes, business models, etc.

Radical innovations, on the other hand, involve fundamental innovations and changes based on revolutionary ideas or technologies. They can affect new products, services, processes, business models or the organisation of the company.

text.text: To what extent do you believe that the potential of the innovation supported by the project will be disruptive on the market in the long term, even if the innovation is not (yet) implemented?

hintText.text: Disruptive innovations create completely new markets with new rules of the game and new target groups, and/or succeed in pushing previously successful organisations or existing technologies and applications from the market with innovative products, services, processes or business models.

text.text: Direct results from the supported project
TRL/SRL 1 – Observation and description of the basic principle of a technology, solution, approach or application. Basic research is recorded and presented.
TRL/SRL 2 – Technology, solution or application concept formulated (identification of possible applications). Description of the application.
TRL/SRL 3 – Experimental or analytical proof of concept of the proposed technology, solution or approach.
TRL/SRL 4 – Technology, solution or approach validated in a laboratory, simulated or smale scale field environment. Test setup in the laboratory or in the field.
TRL/SRL 5 – Experimental development. Test set-up, technology, solution or approach validated in a relevant or operational environment.
TRL/SRL 6 – Technology, solution or approach demonstrated or tested in a relevant environment.
TRL/SRL 7 – System, prototype or solution demonstrated in a development or pilot environment. Prototype or solution in use.
TRL/SRL 8 – System or solution complete and qualified with proof of functionality in operational or real-world conditions.

TRL/SRL 9 – Qualified system or solution has been proven in the operational environment with evidence of successful use or implementation in practice. Production under competitive conditions or sustained implementation in real world settings.
TRL/SRL does not apply to my project / no answer

name.text: Application oriented research / Applied research / Experimental development
Demonstration and pilot plants / Prototypes / Solution or approach demonstrated

text.text: Which Technology Readiness Level (TRL) or Social Readiness Level (SRL) best describes the maturity of your project at the beginning of the Eurostars funding?

notAvailableChoiceText.text: TRL/SRL does not apply to my project / no answer

text.text: TRL/SRL 1 – Observation and description of the basic principle of a technology, solution, approach or application. Basic research is recorded and presented.
TRL/SRL 2 – Technology, solution or application concept formulated (identification of possible applications). Description of the application.
TRL/SRL 3 – Experimental or analytical proof of concept of the proposed technology, solution or approach.
TRL/SRL 4 – Technology, solution or approach validated in a laboratory, simulated or smale scale field environment. Test setup in the laboratory or in the field.
TRL/SRL 5 – Experimental development. Test set-up, technology, solution or approach validated in a relevant or operational environment.
TRL/SRL 6 – Technology, solution or approach demonstrated or tested in a relevant environment.
TRL/SRL 7 – System, prototype or solution demonstrated in a development or pilot environment. Prototype or solution in use.
TRL/SRL 8 – System or solution complete and qualified with proof of functionality in operational or real-world conditions.

TRL/SRL 9 – Qualified system or solution has been proven in the operational environment with evidence of successful use or implementation in practice. Production under competitive conditions or sustained implementation in real world settings.
TRL/SRL does not apply to my project / no answer

name.text: Application oriented research / Applied research / Experimental development
Demonstration and pilot plants / Prototypes / Solution or approach demonstrated

text.text: Which Technology Readiness Level (TRL) or Social Readiness Level (SRL) best describes the status of your project at the end of the Eurostars funding?

notAvailableChoiceText.text: TRL/SRL does not apply to my project / no answer

text.text:
In-licensing
(rights to use, develop or commercialise intellectual property owned by another organisation)

Out-licensing 
(granting rights to use, develop or commercialise one's own intellectual property to another organisation)
Have the results of the project led to property rights (granted or applied for) or are such rights planned?
The project was not continued or implemented.
The project results were not yet sufficiently advanced to decide on a concrete application.
The feasibility of the project goals had been examined, and concrete implementation steps were possible.
Conclusive results made it possible to take concrete and further implementation steps.
The project or innovation was in the implementation and introduction phase, either on the market or in a societal or institutional context.
The project or the innovation had been successfully implemented and already generates first revenues and/or demonstrates measurable societal benefits or strengthens organisational performance.
How do you assess, at the end of the project, the achieved goal of utilisation of the project results and the further implementation (e.g. commercial, societal or organisational)?

If you have filed any patent applications in relation to your project results, please specify for each of them the title of the patent/s, the application number/s, and the name of the patent office/s:
Have your project results been acquired or licensed by another legal entity?
Other scientific outputs such as books, chapters or reports
​​​​Which and how many scientific outputs has your organisation generated in connection with your project results?

hintText.text: (Write 0 in case you have not developed scientific outputs)

text.text: Collaboration with research organisations and academia
Collaboration with large companies and multi-national corporates
How did your organisation benefit from participating in the project?

hintText.text: Please select all that apply:

text.text:
Collaboration with research organisations and academia
Access to leading expertise in the field
Access to knowledge not available in Switzerland

Collaboration with large companies and multi-national corporates:
Expansion of value chain through new partnerships
Enhancement of existing  value chain though innovation

Collaboration with SME(s)
Expansion of the value chain through new partnerships
Enhancement of existing value chain though innovation

Mitigate R&D and Innovation risks
Risk reduction through direct financial support
For the selected benefits in the last question, please specify which of the following more detailed benefits were important:
Strengthening of an existing partnership with an international implementation partner
Exploration/Establishment of a new partnership with an international implementation partner
Possibility to collaborate exclusively with an implementation partner (no research partner required)
Collaboration with an international research partner
How important were the following criteria for your decision to apply for this specific international programme?

Scientific and technological progress
(research advances, prototypes, validation)

Reduction of technical and implementation risks
(feasibility, proof of concept)

International collaboration and access to expertise/infrastructure
(partnerships, complementary know-how, shared facilities)

Market readiness
(use-cases, early testing with users, relevance for later implementation)

Visibility, credibility and attractiveness for follow-up funding
(strengthening reputation, strategic positioning)

Application orientation of collaboration with research partner
(influence over research direction, ownership of results, e.g. intellectual property, timeliness of collaboration)
Which aspects of your project were strengthened through the direct funding?

hintText.text:
Alternativen:

To what extent did the direct financial support from the international programme strengthen the following aspects of your project?

text.text: Yes, with the same and other partners
Are you still collaborating internationally?
The collaboration continued and still exists today.
The collaboration had continued but has since been terminated.
The collaboration ended with project completion.
Please indicate the status of collaboration with one or more of the original project partners after project completion
Has your organisation employed people from the research partner(s) as a result of the supported project?
Economic key indicators of the organisation
... at the end of the project, i. e. in the year
... today, i.e. in the last completed business year
Net turnover (excluding VAT) from location Switzerland

hintText.text:
Please provide information in millions of CHF.
{{custom.TextFTEUnternehmenAmt}}
Use a dot as decimal separator.
If no turnover has been achieved, please enter CHF 0 million.

Net turnover is defined as the amounts derived from the sale of all your products and the provision of all your services, after deducting returns and/or taxes directly linked to turnover.

Clarifications on the calculation of net turnover:

  • For consulting firms, etc.: Gross fee income

  • For trading companies: Gross margin

  • For non-profit organisations: Total income

text.text: Please indicate the revenue in millions of CHF.

text.text: Direct change, specifically as a result of the project. Please indicate any decreases as negative figures.
Total cost of the project, in #.## million CHF
Additional public funding received by your organisation (excluding funding for this specific project), in #.## million CHF
Additional private funding/own resources (excluding funding for this specific project), in #.## million CHF
Research and development expenditure, in #.# million CHF
Employees in research and development, in full-time equivalents (#.#)
To what extent have these variables changed as a result of the supported project up to the present time?

hintText.text: Please refer your estimation to the current year compared to the year of the project launch and indicate the direct impact from the project.

text.text: Please enter value as millions of CHF.
Has your organisation received any complementary research, development or innovation (R&D&I) funding to exploit or scale up your project results?
Yes, it is in the process of founding

Has your involvement in the project contributed to the creation of a spin-off? 
If so, please add the company's name and/or VAT identifier.

hintText.text: A spin-off is an independent firm created by a parent entity – such as a university or company – to commercialise technology, intellectual property, or research-based know-how.

text.text: Did the supported project or the implemented innovation lead to direct cost savings in your organisation overall?
Was it possible to gain additional market share thanks to the implemented innovation?
Novelty on one or more international markets
Novelty on all international markets (global market)
Further impacts and benefits of the supported project
Accelerated research and development in the project
Accelerated market implementation of the project
Increased knowledge and technology transfer (KTT) with the research partner
Has the project funding by Innosuisse led to the following direct effects in your organisation?
Does the supported project or the innovation since introduced make a specific contribution to ecological and/or social sustainability in the market or for society?

hintText.text:
Environmental sustainability includes e.g. contributions regarding:Social sustainability includes e.g. contributions regarding:
- Climate mitigation and adaptation
- Renewable energy and energy efficiency
- Sustainable mobility
- Sustainable production and consumption, circular economy
- Prevention of environmental pollution (waste, air, water)
- Sustainable use of resources (raw materials, other materials)
- Sustainable water management
- Biodiversity and restoration of habitats (land and water)
- Sustainable design of settlements and cities
- Creation of environmental benefits / reduction of environmental costs
- Social cohesion (expanding soft and hard (public) infrastructure) 
- Reducing gender and other inequalities in the population 
- Social coexistence and participation 
- Quality education - Combating poverty 
- Development promotion and cooperation 
- Creation of social benefits / reduction of social costs

text.text: How do you assess the societal and/or social benefit of the Innosuisse-supported project, outside of your organisation?

hintText.text:
A societal and/or social benefit accrues, for example, if: 

  • the project aims at a (social) innovation for a societal problem or challenge, leads to an improvement in the current situation (more effective, efficient, sustainable or equitable) and creates a societal added value or reduces social costs.
  • the benefit also accrues outside of purely economic criteria (e.g. revenue, profit, market results) and outside of your organisation.

text.text: In simple terms, please describe the outcome of your project and its impact on society and your sector. If your organisation has received media attention because of, or connected with your project results, please include a hyperlink to any articles.
The project would have been carried out in a similar form and timeline
The project would have been carried out but with slight delays or slight reduction in scope
The project would have been carried out but with substantial delays or substantial reduction in scope
The project would have required alternative financing (e.g., private, institutional)
The project would likely not have been carried out
Would you have carried out your innovation project even without the innovation support from Innosuisse / Eureka?

surveyEndText.text:
Innosuisse thanks you for your valuable contribution.

If you want to download your completed questionnaire, please click here: Download PDF.
